California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
John H. Larson 
Chairman 
(916) 322-5901 
June 6, 1988 
Michael B. Montgomery 
Redevelopment Agency Counsel 
City of Huntington Park 
c/o 215 North Marengo Avenue 
Second Floor 
Pasadena, California 91101 
Dear Commissioner Montgomery: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I-88-192 
You have written on behalf of Huntington Park Redevelopment 
Agency Board Member Jim Roberts. 
You have requested that we 
provide informal assistance with regard to possible issues 
raised under the Political Reform Act (the "Act"). y 
QUESTION 
May a redevelopment agency board member become employed by 
a developer who does business in the redevelopment agency area 
where the employment will relate to projects in other 
jurisdictions? 
CONCLUSION 
The Act does not prohibit public officials from engaging in 
private employment. 
The Act does prohibit public officials 
from making, participating in, or using their official 
positions to influence, governmental decisions affecting a 
y 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise 
indicated. 
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code 
of Regulations section 18000, et seg. 
All references to 
regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code 
of Regulations. 
Informal assistance does not provide the 
requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal 
written advice. 
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of 
Regs. Section 18329{c) (3).) 
428 J Street, Suite 800 * P.o. Box 807 * Sacramento CA 95804-0807 


Michael B. Montgomery 
June 6, 1988 
Page -2-
private source of income. 
If the developer has future business 
with the redevelopment agency, disqualification would likely be 
required.~ 
FACTS 
You are counsel for the Huntington Park redevelopment 
agency. 
In that capacity, you have sought our assistance in 
responding to a question posed by one of your clients. 
Mr. Jim 
Roberts is a councilmember and redevelopment agency board 
member in the City of Huntington Park. 
Mr. Roberts is considering possible employment with a 
developer who has in the past done work in the redevelopment 
agency project area. 
The developer currently has a disposition 
and development agreement with the redevelopment agency, which 
was entered into by the agency director. 
Mr. Roberts had no 
involvement in that agreement as an agency board member and has 
no knowledge of the details of the agreement. 
The developer wishes to undertake redevelopment agency 
assisted shopping center projects in other communities, outside 
Huntington Park. 
Mr. Roberts is considering going to work for 
the developer on a commission basis for the purpose of seeking 
development opportunities in these other jurisdictions. 
However, in the future, the developer may have other dealings 
with either the City of Huntington Park or its redevelopment 
agency. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act prohibits a public official from making, 
participating in, or using his official position to influence a 
governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know 
he has a financial interest. 
(Section 87100.) 
Mr. Roberts is 
a public official in both his capacity as a councilmember and 
as a redevelopment agency board member. 
(Section 82048.) 
A public official has a financial interest in a decision if 
the decision will have a reasonably foreseeable material 
financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the public 
generally, on the official, a member of the official's 
immediate family, or on: 
~ If the developer would be entering into a contract with 
either the city or the redevelopment agency, the provisions of 
Government Code section 1090 should be considered. 


Michael B. Montgomery 
June 6, 1988 
Page -3-
. . . (c) 
Any source of income, other than gifts 
and other than loans by a commercial lending 
institution in the regular course of business on terms 
available to the public without regard to official 
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) 
or more in value provided to, received by or promised 
to the public official within 12 months prior to the 
time when the decision is made. 
section 87103 
Consequently, if Mr. Roberts receives or is promised $250 
in income by the developer, he will be required to disqualify 
himself for a period of one year from any participation in a 
governmental decision affecting the developer. 
This would 
include any direct dealings with the developer by either the 
city or the redevelopment agency. 
(Regulation 18702.1.) 
However, the Act does not restrict Mr. Roberts private 
economic activities. 
The Act only affects his role in his 
official capacity with the city and the redevelopment agency. 
Consequently, the Act would not prohibit Mr. Roberts from 
undertaking such employment. 
As a caveat, you and Mr. Roberts 
may wish to consider the possible implications of his situation 
under Government Code section 1090, et seq. 
As you are aware, 
the Commission does not have jurisdiction with regard to that 
section_and does not provide advice with respect to it. 
In addition to the foregoing, Mr. Roberts should remember 
that if disqualification is required, it applies to using his 
official position to influence a governmental decision as well 
as to direct involvement. 
Therefore, he could not contact city 
or redevelopment agency staff relative to a decision involving 
the developer once the developer is a source of income to him. 
I trust that this letter responds to the question you have 
posed. 
Should you or Mr. Roberts have questions about this 
letter, I may be reached at (916) 322-5901.

Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

Dear Mik'e: 
MEMBERS OF CITY COUNCIL

JIM ROBERTS 
WILLIAM P. CUNNINGHAM 
I request a rul i ng fran the Agency Attorney regardi ng my 
employment by a developer that has done business in the City of 
Hunt ington Park. 
I have not accepted nor di scussed any financi al 
arrangements at this time and before I do, I need to know if it is 
1 eg a 1 to do so .. 
The developer currently has one DDA given to him by the Di-
rector, but no act ion has been taken by the Agency and I am not 
aware of the details of the DDA. 
What would be my status? 
JR :jw 
Sincerely,
